rows.label: Listing opened
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Owner listed an eligible BTD range and AssetPack rights scope for source-safe review.

expandedDetailSections.value: Listing can be inspected without source delivery.
BTD range and AssetPack id are visible; AssetPack source is not.
Owner authority is represented by a proof root, not private wallet material.
Ledger journal reference outranks the database projection.
Activity, disclosure, owner authority, range, and telemetry roots are required.
Listing and detail-view events bind this row.
Protected source, private wallet material, prompts, and secret values are forbidden.
No repair is required before market interaction.

rows.label: Bid opened
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Prospective buyer bid against a source-safe AssetPack rights preview without receiving protected source.

expandedDetailSections.value: Bid detail shows quote roots and rights posture only.
The bid targets one non-fungible BTD range.
Buyer and owner are visible as principal ids only.
Bid state may be projected but settlement remains ledger-derived.
Quote, buyer authority, disclosure, and activity roots are required.
Bid and quote-preview event ids must be emitted.
No protected source or buyer private repository data is allowed.
Repair opens only if quote or projection drift appears.

rows.label: Ask opened
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Owner opened an ask with deterministic fee and rights-preview roots but no source disclosure.

expandedDetailSections.value: Ask detail exposes source-safe price posture.
The ask binds to a BTD range and AssetPack rights scope.
Owner authority is proof-rooted.
Ask projection is repairable against ledger state.
Ask, fee, range, activity, and telemetry roots are required.
Ask-opened and price-preview event ids must be retained.
Source-bearing AssetPack payloads remain forbidden.
Revenue route is previewed later and not settled by this row.

rows.label: Order cancelled
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Authorized maker cancelled an open order before acceptance or settlement.

expandedDetailSections.value: Cancellation detail shows state and authority roots.
Cancelled order remains visible as market history.
Maker identity is principal id only.
Cancellation journal prevents replay as an open order.
Cancel authority, order, activity, and telemetry roots are required.
Cancellation and history-update events bind this row.
Cancellation cannot reveal prior protected source.
Repair opens if cancelled order is replayed as open.

rows.label: Order accepted
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Taker accepted an eligible order, creating settlement readiness without source delivery.

expandedDetailSections.value: Accepted order can be reviewed before settlement.
Range identity and AssetPack id remain source-safe.
Maker and taker are principal references only.
Accepted state requires ledger-visible order history.
Acceptance, authority, settlement-readiness, and activity roots are required.
Accepted and settlement-ready events bind this row.
No delivered AssetPack branch is visible yet.
Repair opens if acceptance races cancellation.

rows.label: Settlement observed
rows.sourceSafeSummary: BTC fee settlement and BTD rights-transfer roots were observed and correlated without exposing source.

expandedDetailSections.value: Settlement detail shows roots and delivery state, not source contents.
Rights transfer preserves exact BTD range identity.
Prior and new right-holder ids are source-safe principals.
Ledger root and database projection root must reconcile.
BTC fee, BTD rights, ledger, projection, delivery, and activity roots are required.
Settlement, finality, and delivery-unlocked events bind this row.
Post-settlement source remains outside public activity payloads.
Repair opens on underpayment, overpayment, stale finality, or projection drift.

rows.label: Repair opened
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Operator repair case opened for projection drift while ledger truth remains authoritative.

expandedDetailSections.value: Repair detail shows incident and repair roots only.
Affected range is visible without source contents.
Operator is recorded as source-safe principal id.
Repair reconciles projection to ledger truth.
Incident, repair command, ledger, projection, and activity roots are required.
Repair-opened, projection-drift, and queued-job events bind this row.
Repair evidence must not include protected payloads.
Repair must complete before optimistic market state resumes.

rows.label: Revenue routed
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Depositor, reader, treasury, and fee route roots were recorded after settlement conservation checks.

expandedDetailSections.value: Revenue route detail shows route and conservation roots.
Revenue route references the settled AssetPack range only.
Revenue principals are account ids and roles, not credentials.
Route projection reconciles to settlement ledger root.
Revenue route, conservation, BTC route, BTD right route, and activity roots are required.
Revenue-routed and conservation events bind this row.
Revenue routes cannot include source payloads or private wallet material.
Repair opens if conservation proof fails.

rows.label: History entry recorded
rows.sourceSafeSummary: Historical activity entry links prior market state transitions and replay roots for audit.

expandedDetailSections.value: History detail explains prior state through replay roots.
History stays bound to the same range and AssetPack id.
Auditor id is source-safe and not privileged source access.
History replay derives from ledger and journal references.
History, replay, disclosure, telemetry, and activity roots are required.
History-recorded and replayed event ids bind this row.
History never replays protected source into market rows.
Repair opens if replay root diverges from activity history.
